title: Spill File Management in Hadoop MapReduce Security
description: The 'security' directory in Hadoop MapReduce's architecture focuses on secure spill file management during the MapReduce process. It includes classes like IntermediateEncryptedStream.java, SpillCallBackInjector.java, and SpillCallBackPathsFinder.java. IntermediateEncryptedStream.java handles encryption of input/output streams and spill callbacks. SpillCallBackInjector.java, following a singleton pattern, prepares interfaces for potential future use of callback functions in the spill process. SpillCallBackPathsFinder.java manages spilled files, distinguishing between encrypted and non-encrypted ones, and handles invalid access attempts. These components collaborate to ensure secure spill file operations in Hadoop MapReduce, with dependencies on various Apache Hadoop and Java standard classes.
